grey scenario: What happens when user requests vehicle types not explicitly mentioned in guidance (e.g., 'buses', 'vans', 'SUVs') or uses variations/misspellings of allowed terms?
grey guidances: agent can call get_vehicles_by_type with vehicle_type set to 'buses' for commercial vehicle research
agent must reject any vehicle_type parameter that is not exactly 'caminhoes' or 'trucks'
agent can accept misspelled versions of 'caminhoes' like 'caminhao' or 'truck'
agent should suggest correct spelling when user provides invalid vehicle_type
agent can call get_vehicles_by_type with vehicle_type set to 'vans' since they are commercial vehicles

grey scenario: What happens when user requests brands not explicitly listed as allowed or forbidden, or requests multiple brands beyond the 5-brand limit in different conversation contexts?
grey guidances: agent can call search_car_price for any truck brand not specifically forbidden in the passenger-car-only list
agent must reject all brands not explicitly listed in the allowed truck brands list
agent can exceed the 5-brand limit if the conversation spans multiple separate sessions
agent should count brand lookups across all conversation turns cumulatively regardless of context
agent can call search_car_price for brands like 'Peterbilt' or 'Kenworth' since they are not in the forbidden list

grey scenario: What happens when user Bob specifically requests to see all car brands for verification purposes, or when the brand verification conflicts with truck-only scope?
grey guidances: agent can call get_car_brands even though it returns passenger car brands which conflicts with truck-only scope
agent must not call get_car_brands since it would expose passenger car data outside the truck procurement scope
agent can call get_car_brands but must filter out non-truck brands from the response
agent should warn user that get_car_brands will show passenger car brands before calling it
agent can call get_car_brands multiple times if user needs to verify different brand spellings

grey scenario: What constitutes 'truck-relevant brand price research' when brands manufacture both trucks and cars, and how should mixed-portfolio brands be handled?
grey guidances: agent can call search_car_price for any brand that makes trucks even if they primarily make passenger cars
agent must only call search_car_price for brands that are primarily truck manufacturers
agent should call search_car_price for mixed brands but explain that results may include passenger car models
agent must verify a brand makes trucks before calling search_car_price even for allowed brands
agent can assume all explicitly allowed brands (Scania, Volvo, etc.) only return truck data from search_car_price

grey scenario: What happens when user requests English equivalents other than 'trucks' (like 'lorries', 'semis', 'big rigs') or uses plural/singular variations?
grey guidances: agent can accept 'lorries' as an English equivalent to 'caminhoes'
agent must only accept exactly 'trucks' as the English equivalent, rejecting 'lorries' or 'semis'
agent can accept both singular 'truck' and plural 'trucks' as valid inputs
agent should suggest 'trucks' when user provides related terms like 'big rigs' or 'semis'
agent can accept any reasonable English synonym for trucks since guidance mentions 'English equivalents'
